description: Seed corpus for the RAG service — full ticker coverage for NVDA, ESLT, NXSN, TOND, CUE, GOOGL, SPCX, ^VIX, UVXY.

documents.title: Nvidia Q1-2026 results: data-center momentum continues
documents.text: Nvidia reported data-center revenue of $41.2B for Q1-2026, up 38% year over year, with total revenue of $48.9B. Management raised full-year guidance citing sustained demand for Blackwell-generation accelerators and early Rubin pre-orders. Gross margin held at 74%. CFO commentary flagged supply constraints easing in H2 and $12B in new long-term supply agreements.

documents.title: NVDA options flow ahead of June expiry
documents.text: Open interest in NVDA June call options at the 1300 strike rose 4x in two weeks, while put/call ratio fell to 0.42. Implied volatility for the front month sits at 52%, the highest since the January 2026 selloff. Dealers are short gamma above 1280, which could accelerate moves through that level into expiry.

documents.title: Hyperscaler capex tracker — Q2-2026
documents.text: Aggregate hyperscaler capital expenditure guidance for 2026 was revised up 9% to $385B, with AI infrastructure the dominant line item. Nvidia's share of accelerator shipments is estimated at 81%, down from 86% a year earlier as custom silicon ramps. Pricing on flagship parts remains firm; lead times average 22 weeks.

documents.title: Elbit Systems wins $760M European munitions contract
documents.text: Elbit Systems announced a $760M contract to supply precision-guided munitions to a European NATO member over five years. The order backlog now stands at a record $22.6B, with Europe contributing 41% of new orders. Management noted production capacity expansions in Ramat HaSharon and a new facility in Romania coming online in Q3-2026.

documents.title: Elbit Q4-2025: margins expand on backlog execution
documents.text: Elbit Systems closed FY-2025 with revenue of $7.4B, up 12%, and operating margin of 9.1%, up 140 basis points. Free cash flow turned positive at $410M after two years of working-capital build. Guidance for 2026 implies 10-13% revenue growth. Management cited European rearmament programs and UAS demand as primary drivers.

documents.title: Next Vision Q1-2026: record quarter on drone-camera demand
documents.text: Next Vision Stabilized Systems reported Q1-2026 revenue of $48M, up 71% year over year, with net margin of 47%. The company supplies stabilized micro-cameras for tactical drones; management disclosed a design win with two additional Tier-1 European UAS integrators. Order backlog reached $96M, roughly double the prior-year level.

documents.title: Tondo Smart wins smart-lighting tender for 12 Italian municipalities
documents.text: Tondo Smart, an Israeli smart-city IoT company traded on the TASE, won a framework tender to deploy its lighting-control platform across 12 Italian municipalities, an estimated NIS 38M over four years. The deal more than doubles Tondo's contracted backlog. Management reiterated a path to EBITDA breakeven by Q4-2026.

documents.title: Cue Biopharma reports interim Phase 1b data for CUE-101
documents.text: Cue Biopharma released interim Phase 1b data for CUE-101 in HPV16+ head and neck cancer: overall response rate of 36% in the combination arm versus 19% historical control, with a manageable safety profile. Enrollment completion is expected in Q4-2026. Cash of $54M funds operations into mid-2027 after April's $40M raise.

documents.title: CUE options pricing a binary catalyst
documents.text: CUE January 2027 options imply a move of ±85% around the expected Phase 1b readout, with call skew steepening over the past month. Short interest stands at 14% of float. Desk flags wide bid-ask spreads and recommends defined-risk structures such as call spreads over outright premium for catalyst positioning.

documents.title: Alphabet Q1-2026: Search resilient, Cloud accelerating
documents.text: Alphabet reported Q1-2026 revenue of $90.2B, up 12% YoY, beating estimates. Google Search revenue grew 10% to $50.7B, defying fears of AI search disruption. Google Cloud surged 28% to $12.3B with operating margin expanding to 17.1%. YouTube ad revenue hit $9.0B (+11%). Gemini AI integration across Search, Workspace and Cloud is being cited as a key growth driver for H2-2026.

documents.title: GOOGL: AI search moat intact, Cloud thesis strengthening
documents.text: Google's AI Overviews (formerly SGE) now cover 35% of search queries with no meaningful ad revenue erosion detected. Analyst consensus has raised 2026 EPS estimates by 8% post-earnings. The Cloud unit is on track to reach $50B annualized run-rate by Q4-2026. Key risk: DOJ antitrust remedy hearings in Q3; structural separation of Chrome or Android remains a tail risk. Price target raised to $210.

documents.title: GOOGL options: low implied vol relative to DOJ event risk
documents.text: GOOGL 30-day implied volatility at 24% sits near a 52-week low despite pending DOJ antitrust remedy decision expected in Q3. Put/call ratio at 0.68 suggests the market is not pricing the structural remedy tail risk adequately. The desk recommends ratio put spreads to define downside exposure into the ruling. Support levels cluster at $155 and $140.

documents.title: SpaceX Starship Flight 9 achieves full mission success
documents.text: SpaceX successfully completed Starship Flight 9 with full booster catch and upper stage ocean splashdown, validating the full reusability architecture. NASA confirmed Starship HLS readiness for the Artemis III lunar landing scheduled for late 2026. Starlink constellation surpassed 7,000 active satellites with 4.2M subscribers globally, generating an estimated $8B annualized revenue. The milestone significantly de-risks the SPCX space ETF underlying holdings.

documents.title: Space economy ETF outlook: SPCX positioned for Starlink IPO
documents.text: The Procure Space ETF (SPCX) has 23% of its NAV in companies with direct SpaceX supply-chain exposure. An anticipated Starlink IPO in late 2026 or 2027 could be the largest tech listing since Alibaba. SPCX also holds significant positions in Planet Labs, Rocket Lab, and Intuitive Machines, all benefiting from record NASA commercial contracts. Space sector revenue expected to reach $1T globally by 2030.

documents.title: SpaceX Q2-2026 launch cadence: 28 missions completed
documents.text: SpaceX completed 28 orbital missions in Q2-2026, maintaining its target of 120+ launches for full year 2026. Falcon 9 booster B1058 set a new reuse record at 25 flights. The Starship program is preparing for Flight 10 targeting full orbital re-entry and Texas landing. Commercial crew missions for NASA and private customers continue at record pace, supporting SPCX ETF constituent revenue growth.

documents.title: VIX market analysis: elevated uncertainty regime
documents.text: The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) reflects the market's expectation of 30-day S&P 500 volatility. A VIX above 20 signals elevated uncertainty; above 30 signals stress; above 40 signals panic. Current VIX term structure: check live data for current regime. Historically, VIX spikes above 30 have represented buying opportunities in equities with average forward 12-month SPX returns of +18%. Mean reversion of VIX from elevated levels typically occurs within 3-6 months.

documents.title: VIX term structure: contango vs backwardation dynamics
documents.text: VIX term structure analysis: when VIX9D (9-day) > VIX (30-day) > VIX3M (3-month), the market is in backwardation — a signal of acute near-term fear and potential for sharp reversals. Normal market state (contango) has VIX3M > VIX > VIX9D. Contango implies the market expects volatility to decline. UVXY profits from VIX spikes but suffers severe decay in contango. Volatility carry trades (short UVXY) work 75% of calendar days in normal contango regimes.

documents.title: VIX options and hedging strategies for the current regime
documents.text: VIX call options (which hedge equity portfolios against volatility spikes) are priced with a persistent positive skew — OTM calls are expensive relative to puts due to the asymmetric risk profile of volatility. S&P 500 portfolio managers typically hold 3-5% of NAV in VIX calls or UVXY as a hedge. VIX futures roll yield (cost of maintaining a long VIX position) averages -5% to -8% monthly in contango, making UVXY a costly long-term hold. Active management of hedge ratio is critical.

documents.title: UVXY: 1.5x leveraged VIX short-term futures ETF mechanics
documents.text: UVXY (ProShares Ultra VIX Short-Term Futures ETF) provides 1.5x daily leveraged exposure to the S&P 500 VIX Short-Term Futures Index, which rolls between the front two VIX futures months. Due to daily rebalancing and futures roll decay, UVXY is designed as a short-term trading instrument, not a long-term hold. In normal contango markets, UVXY loses 5-10% of its value per month from roll decay alone. It is effective as a hedge during sharp, acute VIX spikes exceeding 5 points.

documents.title: UVXY trading strategy: catalyst-driven spike plays
documents.text: UVXY is best used as a catalyst-driven hedge, not a passive long. Effective entry points: (1) when VIX is below 15 (cheap hedge) before known risk events (FOMC, CPI, earnings), (2) when VIX term structure inverts to backwardation, signaling acute fear. Exit strategy: scale out as VIX spikes above 25-30. Common strategy: buy UVXY calls 2-4 weeks before known catalysts, sell into the event. Avoid holding UVXY through prolonged calm periods due to theta decay compounding with roll decay.

documents.title: UVXY options structure: leveraged exposure to volatility of volatility
documents.text: UVXY options carry extremely high implied volatility (often 80-120% IV) reflecting both the underlying's volatility and the daily rebalancing uncertainty. Call options on UVXY are popular for asymmetric tail-risk hedges. Put options on UVXY are popular as a volatility carry trade — selling UVXY puts generates premium from the persistent roll decay. Risk: a sudden VIX spike can cause UVXY puts to lose significant value rapidly. Position sizing should be conservative, typically under 2% of portfolio notional.
